assertions.title: OpenClaw manifest matches implemented API routes

steps.observed: Routes at lines 352-363 match manifest: health(GET), auth/login(POST), ws(GET), courses(GET), lectures(GET), jobs(POST/GET), jobs/{id}(GET/DELETE)

evidence.curlOutput: All routes respond with expected HTTP status codes and error codes

assertions.notes: All manifest-declared routes exist with correct methods. Public routes (/health, /auth/login) work without auth. Protected routes require Bearer token. Route registration matches openclaw-manifest.json exactly.

assertions.title: API error-code contract matches runtime behavior

evidence.curlOutput: All tested error codes match docs/error-codes.md
evidence.codeInspection: docs/error-codes.md defines all error codes with correct HTTP status codes

assertions.notes: All documented error codes (INVALID_REQUEST, MISSING_TOKEN, INVALID_TOKEN_FORMAT, INVALID_TOKEN, AUTH_FAILED) match runtime behavior. HTTP status codes match documented values. Unit tests cover additional codes like JOB_NOT_FOUND, JOB_CANNOT_CANCEL, INVALID_JOB_CONFIG.

assertions.title: API endpoint response shapes are explicitly consistent or intentionally mixed

steps.observed: Docs explicitly note: /health=bare object, /courses|/lectures|/jobs=bare arrays, /auth/login and DELETE /jobs=respondWithSuccess envelope, POST/GET jobs=Job object directly
CORS headers present: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *, Access-Control-Allow-Headers: Authorization, Content-Type, X-Request-ID, Access-Control-Expose-Headers: X-Request-ID

evidence.curlOutput: Response shapes match documented behavior in api-reference.md
evidence.docsComparison: docs/api-reference.md correctly documents mixed response shapes
evidence.headers: X-Request-ID and CORS headers working correctly

assertions.notes: The API uses intentionally mixed response shapes: bare objects/arrays for data endpoints, envelope format for errors and auth/delete responses. This is explicitly documented in api-reference.md under 'Response formats' section. X-Request-ID correlation works. CORS allows X-Request-ID header.

assertions.title: CLI JSON automation contract matches runtime behavior

steps.observed: Identical output to 'version --json' - flag order independence confirmed

evidence.terminalOutput: CLI --json flag produces deterministic envelope structure

assertions.notes: CLI JSON envelope is deterministic with success/data/error/meta structure. Flag order independence works. Unknown commands return structured error. serve --json is non-blocking as documented. Unit tests comprehensively verify envelope contracts.

summary: Tested 4 assertions: all passed. OpenClaw manifest routes match implementation exactly. Error codes match runtime behavior with correct HTTP status codes. Response shapes are intentionally mixed and explicitly documented. CLI JSON automation contract is fully deterministic and matches both docs and unit tests.
